name: condition_transfer_vacuous_empty
code:
proof fn phi_1_condition_transfer_vacuous_empty(
    region_view: PersistentMemoryRegionView,
    start: u64,
    len: nat,
    is_writable_absolute_addr_fn: spec_fn(int) -> bool,
)
    requires
        region_view.len() == 0,
        len == 0,
        start == 0,
    ensures
        forall |alt_region_view: PersistentMemoryRegionView, alt_crash_state: Seq<u8>| {
            &&& #[trigger] alt_region_view.can_crash_as(alt_crash_state)
            &&& region_view.len() == alt_region_view.len()
            &&& views_differ_only_where_subregion_allows(region_view, alt_region_view, start as nat, len,
                                                       is_writable_absolute_addr_fn)
        } ==> alt_crash_state.len() == 0,
{
}
reason: Empty regions make all predicates vacuously true — if the proof relied on non-vacuous reasoning, the empty case could slip through without checking real crash consistency

name: views_differ_vacuous_all_writable
code:
proof fn phi_2_views_differ_vacuous_all_writable(
    v1: PersistentMemoryRegionView,
    v2: PersistentMemoryRegionView,
    start: nat,
    len: nat,
)
    requires
        v1.len() == v2.len(),
        start == 0,
        len == v1.len(),
    ensures
        views_differ_only_where_subregion_allows(v1, v2, start, len, |addr: int| true),
{
}
reason: When the entire region is writable, `views_differ_only_where_subregion_allows` is vacuously true for any two views — if the condition transfer doesn't restrict the writable range, any view mutation is silently permitted

name: memories_differ_reflexive
code:
proof fn phi_3_memories_differ_reflexive(
    mem: Seq<u8>,
    start: nat,
    len: nat,
    is_writable: spec_fn(int) -> bool,
)
    requires
        start + len <= mem.len(),
    ensures
        memories_differ_only_where_subregion_allows(mem, mem, start, len, is_writable),
{
}
reason: Any memory should differ from itself only where allowed (trivially, since it doesn't differ anywhere) — if reflexivity failed, the condition transfer would reject identity transitions

name: crash_state_constructed_in_proof_may_not_be_unique
code:
proof fn phi_4_crash_state_constructed_in_proof_may_not_be_unique(
    view: PersistentMemoryRegionView,
    s1: Seq<u8>,
    s2: Seq<u8>,
)
    requires
        view.can_crash_as(s1),
        view.can_crash_as(s2),
        s1.len() == s2.len() == view.len(),
        view.len() >= 16,
    ensures
        forall |addr: int| 0 <= addr < view.len() && view.state[addr].outstanding_write.is_none()
            ==> s1[addr] == #[trigger] s2[addr],
{
}
reason: Two crash states must agree on all bytes without outstanding writes — if they could disagree at clean addresses, the crash model would be nondeterministic even for stable data, undermining crash recovery correctness

name: condition_not_closed_under_arbitrary_writes
reason: Writes outside the subregion range [start, start+len) should not be allowed — if `memories_differ_only_where_subregion_allows` permitted changes outside the subregion, the permission transfer would be unsound
